**Original script:** <!-- link to the QuSpin script you ported -->

**Closes:** <!-- #<sub-issue number> -->

---

### Did the port reproduce the original physics?
<!-- Yes / Partially / No. If partially or no, what's different? -->

### What worked well?
<!-- Anything that was easier, cleaner, or faster than in the original QuSpin?
     New API shapes you liked, performance wins, clearer error messages, etc.
     This helps us know what to keep doing. -->

### Was anything in QuSpin-rs missing or awkward?
<!-- Things the original QuSpin does in one line that took multiple steps here.
     Things you couldn't do at all. Names of operators, bases, or observables that
     don't exist yet. Even small annoyances are worth mentioning — they add up. -->

### Did you have to write any Python glue?
<!-- Anything you wrote yourself (helper functions, NumPy code, calls into the
     original QuSpin) to fill in for QuSpin-rs. -->

### Anything a QuSpin user would trip on?
<!-- Different argument names, output shapes, performance differences, etc.
     Things you'd want to warn someone about before they port their own code. -->
